lot_facts.sb840_note: SB 840 applies to zones permitting commercial/office/retail/warehouse uses. SF-3 is a residential base zone. Not applicable.
lot_facts.hb24_note: HB 24 governs zoning-change legislative procedures — not a plan review development standard.

preemption_summary: No Texas state law preemption is triggered on any finding. The lot is 7,330 sq ft — nearly double the 4,000 sq ft threshold for SB 15 small-lot provisions. All setback, parking, open-space, and FAR findings that could potentially be affected by SB 15 fall under small-lot-only provisions that do not reach this lot. SB 840 does not apply to SF-3 residential zoning. HB 24 is not a plan-check standard.

findings.notes: Lot area documentation accuracy — not addressed by any state statute.
Use type designation — not addressed by state statute.
Ordinance citation on cover sheet — not addressed by state statute.

SB 15 §211.055(a)(4) caps open space requirement at 30% for small lots only (≤4,000 sf). This lot is 7,330 sf — small-lot provision does not apply. HOME Phase I 40% front yard impervious limit governs.
SB 15 §211.055(a)(1) caps front setback at 15 ft for small lots only (≤4,000 sf). Not applicable here. Phase I 15 ft front setback governs and equals the state cap regardless.
SB 15 §211.055(a)(1) caps rear setback at 10 ft for small lots only. Not applicable. Phase I 5 ft rear setback (with alley) is more permissive than the state floor — no conflict.
SB 15 §211.055(a)(1) caps side setback at 5 ft for small lots only. Not applicable. Local 5 ft side setback equals state floor — no conflict even if applicable.
Porch setbacks are not wall articulation requirements. SB 15 §211.055(a)(5) prohibits wall articulation requirements on small lots only. Not applicable here.

SB 15 §211.055(a)(2) prohibits covered parking requirements for small lots. SB 15 §211.055(a)(3) caps parking at 1 per unit for small lots. Neither applies to this 7,330 sf lot. Phase I maximum 4 spaces in front yard is a cap, not a minimum — not preempted.

SB 15 §211.055(a)(6) prohibits building bulk restrictions for small lots only. SB 840 FAR preemption applies to commercial zones only. Neither applies here. Phase I §25-2-773(E) Subchapter F FAR limits govern.
Street-facing entrance requirement is not a wall articulation standard. SB 15 §211.055(a)(7) prohibits wall articulation requirements for small lots only. Not applicable.
IBC structural standards — not addressed by SB 15, SB 840, or HB 24.
